% Grid dimensions
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
rows(Rcnt) :- Rcnt = max_row + 1.
cols(Ccnt) :- Ccnt = max_col + 1.

% Border cells
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = max_row.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = max_col.

% Border non-zero colours
border_nonzero(V) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Smallest border non-zero colour (wall)
wall(W) :- border_nonzero(W), #count{V : border_nonzero(V), V < W} = 0.

% Source cells: border cells whose colour != wall
source(V,R,C) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != wall.

% Flood fill reachable cells (with source colour V)
reachable(V,R0,C0) :- source(V,R0,C0), input(R0,C0,V), V != wall.
reachable(V,R2,C2) :- reachable(V,R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
                       R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                       input(R2,C2,X), X != wall,
                       reachable(V,R1,C1).

% Zero cells reachable
zero_reachable(V,R,C) :- reachable(V,R,C), input(R,C,0).

% Any zero cell reached (exists some source)
any_zero(R,C) :- zero_reachable(V,R,C).

% Interior zero cells (zero not reached)
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not any_zero(R,C).

% Output cell predicate
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour per position
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Constraints for correct colours

% 1. Non-zero cells keep original colour
:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% 2. Zero cells reachable become colour of their source
:- zero_reachable(V,R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% 3. Interior zero cells become wall+1
:- interior_zero(R,C), wall(W), wp1(WP1), WP1 = W + 1, output(R,C,Color), Color != WP1.
```

% Grid dimensions
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
rows(Rcnt) :- Rcnt = max_row + 1.
cols(Ccnt) :- Ccnt = max_col + 1.

% Border cells
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = max_row.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = max_col.

% Border non-zero colours
border_nonzero(V) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Wall colour: smallest border non-zero colour
wall(W) :- border_nonzero(W), #count{V : border_nonzero(V), V < W} = 0.

% Source cells for flood fill
source(V,R,C) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != wall.

% Flood fill reachable cells (with source colour V)
reachable(V,R0,C0) :- source(V,R0,C0), input(R0,C0,V), V != wall.
reachable(V,R2,C2) :- reachable(V,R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
                       R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                       input(R2,C2,X), X != wall,
                       reachable(V,R1,C1).

% Zero cells reached by flood fill
zero_reachable(V,R,C) :- reachable(V,R,C), input(R,C,0).

% Any zero cell reached
any_zero(R,C) :- zero_reachable(V,R,C).

% Interior zero cells (zero not reached)
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not any_zero(R,C).

% Output cell predicate
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour per position
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Constraints

% Non-zero cells keep original colour
:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% Zero cells reachable keep source colour
:- zero_reachable(V,R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% Interior zeros become wall+1
:- interior_zero(R,C), wall(W), wp1(WP1), WP1 = W + 1, output(R,C,Color), Color != WP1.
```

% ARC-AGI transformation: flood fill from border cells with colour != smallest border colour
% non-zero cells keep original colour, reachable zeros become source colour, interior zeros become wall+1

% -------- grid dimensions ----------
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
rows(N) :- N = max_row + 1.
cols(M) :- M = max_col + 1.

% -------- border cells ----------
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = max_row.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = max_col.

% -------- border non‑zero colours ----------
border_nonzero(V) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% -------- smallest border colour (wall) ----------
wall(W) :- border_nonzero(W), #count{V : border_nonzero(V), V < W} = 0.

% -------- source cells (border cells whose colour ≠ wall) ----------
source(V,R,C) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != W.

% -------- allowed cells (cannot have colour = wall) ----------
allowed(R,C) :- input(R,C,X), X != W.

% -------- flood‑fill reachable cells (with source colour V) ----------
reachable(V,R0,C0) :- source(V,R0,C0), allowed(R0,C0).
reachable(V,R2,C2) :- reachable(V,R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
                       R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                       allowed(R2,C2),
                       reachable(V,R1,C1).

% -------- zero cells reached by the flood‑fill ----------
zero_reachable(V,R,C) :- reachable(V,R,C), input(R,C,0).

% -------- any zero cell reached ----------
any_zero(R,C) :- zero_reachable(V,R,C).

% -------- interior zero cells (zero not reachable) ----------
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not any_zero(R,C).

% -------- output cell predicate ----------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour per position
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% -------- 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets ----------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% -------- auxiliary colour for interior zeros ----------
wp1(WP1) :- wall(W), WP1 = W + 1.

% -------- constraints to force correct colour ----------
% Non‑zero cells keep original colour
:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% Zero cells reachable keep source colour
:- zero_reachable(V,R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% Interior zeros become wall+1
:- interior_zero(R,C), wall(W), wp1(WP1), output(R,C,Color), Color != WP1.
```

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI transformation:
%  * Determine the smallest border colour (the “wall”)
%  * Flood‑fill starts at every border cell whose colour ≠ wall
%    and spreads only through cells whose colour ≠ wall.
%  * A zero cell that is reached becomes the colour of the flood‑fill
%    source (the source colour is the colour of the border cell that
%    started the flood).  A zero cell that cannot be reached becomes
%    wall+1.
%  * All other cells keep their original colour.
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% -------- grid dimensions ------------------------------------------
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
rows(Rcnt) :- Rcnt = max_row + 1.
cols(Ccnt) :- Ccnt = max_col + 1.

% -------- border cells (outermost row or column) ------------------
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = max_row.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = max_col.

% -------- border cells that are non‑zero -------------------------
border_nonzero(V) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% -------- smallest border colour (the “wall”) --------------------
wall(W) :- border_nonzero(W), #count{V : border_nonzero(V), V < W} = 0.

% -------- source cells (border cells whose colour ≠ wall) ---------
source(V,R,C) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != W.

% -------- cells that may be used in flood‑fill (colour ≠ wall) ----
allowed(R,C) :- input(R,C,X), X != W.

% -------- flood‑fill reachable cells (with source colour V) -------
reachable(V,R0,C0) :- source(V,R0,C0), allowed(R0,C0).
reachable(V,R2,C2) :- reachable(V,R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
                       R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                       allowed(R2,C2),
                       reachable(V,R1,C1).

% -------- zero cells that are reached by the flood‑fill ----------
zero_reachable(V,R,C) :- reachable(V,R,C), input(R,C,0).

% -------- any zero cell reached (exists a source) ----------------
any_zero(R,C) :- zero_reachable(V,R,C).

% -------- interior zero cells (zero not reachable) ----------------
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not any_zero(R,C).

% -------- output cell definition ----------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour per position
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% -------- 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets -----------------------
delta(0,1).   delta(0,-1).   delta(1,0).   delta(-1,0).

% -------- colour that interior zeros become (wall+1) ------------
wp1(WP1) :- wall(W), WP1 = W + 1.

% -------- constraints to enforce the correct colour ---------------
% 1. non‑zero cells keep their original colour
:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% 2. zero cells reached by the flood‑fill keep the source colour
:- zero_reachable(V,R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% 3. interior zeros become wall+1
:- interior_zero(R,C), wp1(WP1), output(R,C,Color), Color != WP1.
```

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI transformation:
%  * Determine the smallest border colour (the “wall”)
%  * Flood‑fill starts at every border cell whose colour ≠ wall
%    and spreads only through cells whose colour ≠ wall.
%  * A zero cell that is reached becomes the colour of the flood‑fill
%    source (the source colour is the colour of the border cell that
%    started the flood).  A zero cell that cannot be reached becomes
%    wall+1.
%  * All other cells keep their original colour.
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% -------- grid dimensions ------------------------------------------
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
rows(Rcnt) :- Rcnt = max_row + 1.
cols(Ccnt) :- Ccnt = max_col + 1.

% -------- border cells (outermost row or column) ------------------
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = max_row.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = max_col.

% -------- border cells that are non‑zero -------------------------
border_nonzero(V) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% -------- smallest border colour (the “wall”) --------------------
wall(W) :- border_nonzero(W), #count{V : border_nonzero(V), V < W} = 0.

% -------- source cells (border cells whose colour ≠ wall) ---------
source(V,R,C) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != W.

% -------- cells that may be used in flood‑fill (colour ≠ wall) ----
allowed(R,C) :- input(R,C,X), X != W.

% -------- flood‑fill reachable cells (with source colour V) -------
reachable(V,R0,C0) :- source(V,R0,C0), allowed(R0,C0).
reachable(V,R2,C2) :- reachable(V,R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
                       R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                       allowed(R2,C2),
                       reachable(V,R1,C1).

% -------- zero cells that are reached by the flood‑fill ----------
zero_reachable(V,R,C) :- reachable(V,R,C), input(R,C,0).

% -------- any zero cell reached (exists a source) ----------------
any_zero(R,C) :- zero_reachable(V,R,C).

% -------- interior zero cells (zero not reachable) ----------------
interior_zero(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), not any_zero(R,C).

% -------- output cell definition ----------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour per position
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% -------- 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets -----------------------
delta(0,1).   delta(0,-1).   delta(1,0).   delta(-1,0).

% -------- colour that interior zeros become (wall+1) ------------
wp1(WP1) :- wall(W), WP1 = W + 1.

% -------- constraints to enforce the correct colour ---------------
% 1. non‑zero cells keep their original colour
:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% 2. zero cells reached by the flood‑fill keep the source colour
:- zero_reachable(V,R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != V.

% 3. interior zeros become wall+1
:- interior_zero(R,C), wp1(WP1), output(R,C,Color), Color != WP1.
